species: 22-species synthetic subgingival microbiome community (incl. Porphyromonas gingivalis W83, Fusobacterium nucleatum, Actinomyces oris T14V,

growth_records.medium: mucin-serum medium (chemostat), P. gingivalis-based low dilution-rate protocol
growth_records.cite: Li et al. Nutritional pressure from serum amplifies dysbiotic features in an oral microbiome synthetic community. The ISME Journal (2026). DOI:10.1093/ismejo/wrag070. [PMC13140499]
growth_records.snippet: The second protocol was based on the experimental growth rate of P. gingivalis in mucin-serum (Td = 15 h), using a D = 0.0462 h-1, and a F = 23.21 ml h-1.
growth_records.notes: Chemostat steady-state community growth rate = imposed D = 0.0462 h-1, so mu = 0.0462 h-1 (mu = D); Td = 15 h (ln2/0.0462 = 15.0 h, consistent). Flow F = 23.21 mL/h. Temperature 37 C, pH 7.15 +/- 0.15 controlled; microaerophilic 2% O2. Serum varied 0/5/50% v/v. Community-level rate; no numeric per-species or flux data (metabolomics/acid-consumption graphical only).
